steps.title: Find the median position in the data set
steps.content: There are 31 days in December, so there are 31 temperature recordings. The median is the middle value when data is ordered, which occurs at position $\frac{31 + 1}{2} = 16$. We need to find the 16th temperature.

steps.title: Use cumulative frequencies to locate the 16th value
steps.content:
Add up frequencies from the lowest interval until reaching the 16th value:
  • $−5$ to $4$: 1 value (cumulative total: 1)
  • $5$ to $14$: 4 values (cumulative total: $1 + 4 = 5$)
  • $15$ to $24$: 5 values (cumulative total: $5 + 5 = 10$)
  • $25$ to $34$: 6 values (cumulative total: $10 + 6 = 16$)
The 16th value is in the $25$ to $34$ interval, so the median falls in this range.

steps.title: Identify all possible median values
steps.content: Since we only know the median lies in the $25$ to $34$ interval but do not know the exact recorded temperatures within that range, the median could be any integer value from 25 through 34 degrees Fahrenheit. The possible values are: 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 31, 32, 33, and 34.
